articles.title: Baptism record at Manchester Cathedral offers insight into Black Mancunian life in Georgian-era England
articles.description: A parish entry reveals an argument that proved pivotal to the abolitionist cause, at a time when an estimated 20,000 Black people were living in the country

more.description: Former Bafana assistant Serame Letsoaka advises SAFA to retain Hugo Broos temporarily, ensuring a smooth transition and continuity for the national team’s upcoming qualifiers.

more.title: UK Promoter Y2K Entertainment Cuts All Ties With South African Music Industry Over Migrant Crisis Backlash
more.description: South Africa’s music industry has taken a serious international hit. UK-based Y2K Entertainment Promotions, a long-running promoter of Southern African music, announced on 2 July that it is withdrawing all support for South African artists. more.title: Springboks face ticket sales challenges ahead of England Test match
more.description: SA Rugby attributes slow ticket sales for the Springboks' opening Test against England to external factors, including the war in Iran, while captain Siya Kolisi emphasises the team's connection with fans.

more.title: Cash at home: why many South Africans still avoid formal savings
more.description: Nearly half of South Africans continue to save cash at home despite wider access to banking services, exposing themselves to theft, fire and lost interest, according to FinMark Trust and World Bank data. Experts say trust, accessibility and financial habits remain major barriers to formal saving.

more.title: Liberty study reveals financial strain on mid-career South Africans
more.description: A new Liberty and UCT study reveals that South Africans aged 35 to 55 are increasingly anxious about retirement, job security and rising living costs despite being in their highest earning years. The research shows financial resilience has overtaken wealth as the primary measure of success.
